title: Xenial Mediterranean Onion Frittata
description: A resinous and crumbly Mediterranean dish featuring onion, perfect for a brunch mood, made with score fat and Mediterranean friendly ingredients, served in a ramekin.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs and a pinch of salt.
In a large skillet, heat the score fat over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion to the skillet and cook until caramelized, stirring occasionally.
Add the kalamata olives, artichoke hearts, and sun dried tomatoes to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Pour the egg mixture over the onion mixture in the skillet and cook until the edges start to set.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 15 minutes, or until the eggs are almost set.
Remove the skillet from the oven and sprinkle the crumbled feta cheese over the top.
Return the skillet to the oven and bake for an additional 2 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Use a spatula to carefully loosen the edges of the frittata from the skillet and slide it onto a plate.
